# Superflow

> A Claude Code skill that orchestrates a full dev workflow: onboarding, product discovery, autonomous execution with PR-per-sprint, and merge. Hybrid project — markdown prompts + Python companion CLI (supervisor).

## Overview

Superflow is a 4-phase development workflow skill for Claude Code:

- **Phase 0 (Onboarding):** Auto-detects project state and confirms with user, analyzes codebase with 5 parallel agents (4 Claude + 1 Codex), generates health report, creates/audits llms.txt and CLAUDE.md, sets up permissions — structured as 5 modular stage files
- **Phase 1 (Product Discovery):** 12-step interactive brainstorming with dual-model spec/plan review, JTBD user stories, approval gates
- **Phase 2 (Autonomous Execution):** Sprint-based autonomous development — git worktrees, TDD, subagent implementers, specialized 2-agent review (Claude Product + Codex Technical), one PR per sprint
- **Phase 3 (Merge):** User-initiated sequential rebase merge with CI verification and documentation update

Version: v3.3.1 (2026-03-25). MIT License.

## File Structure

- [SKILL.md](SKILL.md): Entry point — startup checklist, provider detection, supervisor detection, phase routing (126 lines)
- [superflow-enforcement.md](superflow-enforcement.md): 9 hard rules that survive context compaction — deployed to ~/.claude/rules/ (80 lines)
- [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md): Project instructions for Claude — architecture, key files table, conventions (111 lines)
- [README.md](README.md): Installation, usage, supervisor docs, file table, permissions setup (162 lines)

## Phase References

- [references/phase0-onboarding.md](references/phase0-onboarding.md): Phase 0 router — detection, recovery matrix, stage loading (~80 lines)
- [references/phase0/stage1-detect.md](references/phase0/stage1-detect.md): Parallel preflight, auto-detection, confirmation (~214 lines)
- [references/phase0/stage2-analysis.md](references/phase0/stage2-analysis.md): 5 parallel agents, tiered model usage (~256 lines)
- [references/phase0/stage3-report.md](references/phase0/stage3-report.md): Health report, informative summary, approval (~179 lines)
- [references/phase0/stage4-setup.md](references/phase0/stage4-setup.md): 3 concurrent branches, strict file ownership (~238 lines)
- [references/phase0/stage5-completion.md](references/phase0/stage5-completion.md): Markers, tech debt persistence, restart (~163 lines)
- [references/phase0/greenfield.md](references/phase0/greenfield.md): Greenfield path G1-G6 (~350 lines)
- [references/phase1-discovery.md](references/phase1-discovery.md): Product discovery — 12 steps, brainstorm STOP GATE, product summary APPROVAL, spec/plan with dual-model review (132 lines)
- [references/phase2-execution.md](references/phase2-execution.md): Autonomous sprint execution — 10 steps, worktree, TDD, implementer dispatch, 2-agent specialized review (Claude Product + Codex Technical), push+PR, Final Holistic Review (166 lines)
- [references/phase3-merge.md](references/phase3-merge.md): Merge workflow — pre-merge checklist, sequential rebase, CI handling, post-merge report (87 lines)

## Agent Prompts

- [prompts/implementer.md](prompts/implementer.md): TDD-driven code implementation agent — Red-Green-Refactor cycle, test evidence required (81 lines)
- [prompts/spec-reviewer.md](prompts/spec-reviewer.md): Spec compliance reviewer — verifies implementation matches specification (55 lines)
- [prompts/code-quality-reviewer.md](prompts/code-quality-reviewer.md): Code quality reviewer — correctness, security, maintainability, OWASP (77 lines)
- [prompts/product-reviewer.md](prompts/product-reviewer.md): Product acceptance reviewer — user perspective, UX gaps, edge cases (72 lines)
- [prompts/llms-txt-writer.md](prompts/llms-txt-writer.md): llms.txt generation/audit prompt — llmstxt.org standard, verified entries only (156 lines)
- [prompts/claude-md-writer.md](prompts/claude-md-writer.md): CLAUDE.md generation/audit prompt — verified paths and commands (150 lines)
- [prompts/testing-guidelines.md](prompts/testing-guidelines.md): Testing reference — TDD cycle, coverage expectations, included in implementer context (68 lines)
- [prompts/codex/code-reviewer.md](prompts/codex/code-reviewer.md): Codex code quality review prompt
- [prompts/codex/product-reviewer.md](prompts/codex/product-reviewer.md): Codex product acceptance review prompt
- [prompts/codex/audit.md](prompts/codex/audit.md): Codex onboarding audit prompt

## Agent Definitions

12 agent definitions in `agents/` with reasoning tier frontmatter (deep/standard/fast):
- Deep tier (Opus, high effort): deep-implementer, deep-code-reviewer, deep-product-reviewer, deep-spec-reviewer, deep-doc-writer, deep-analyst
- Standard tier (Sonnet, medium effort): standard-implementer, standard-code-reviewer, standard-product-reviewer, standard-spec-reviewer, standard-doc-writer
- Fast tier (Sonnet, low effort): fast-implementer

## Supervisor System

The supervisor is a Python CLI that orchestrates autonomous multi-sprint execution outside of Claude Code's context window.

- [bin/superflow-supervisor](bin/superflow-supervisor): CLI entry point — argparse-based with run/status/resume/reset commands (147 lines)
- [lib/supervisor.py](lib/supervisor.py): Core supervisor — worktree lifecycle, prompt building, sprint execution with retry, run loop, completion report, validation gates, holistic review, enforcement hardening (1733 lines, ~1350 LOC)
- [lib/queue.py](lib/queue.py): Sprint queue — JSON-based queue with DAG dependency resolution, atomic saves, status tracking (114 lines, ~98 LOC)
- [lib/checkpoint.py](lib/checkpoint.py): Checkpoint system — save/load sprint execution state for crash recovery with atomic writes, string IDs, named checkpoints (52 lines, ~44 LOC)
- [lib/parallel.py](lib/parallel.py): Parallel execution — ThreadPoolExecutor-based concurrent sprint execution with queue locking (47 lines, ~38 LOC)
- [lib/replanner.py](lib/replanner.py): Adaptive replanner — analyzes completed sprints, adjusts remaining plans via Claude subprocess (212 lines, ~168 LOC)
- [lib/notifications.py](lib/notifications.py): Notifications — Telegram Bot API integration with stdout fallback, 16 event types (159 lines, ~130 LOC)
- [templates/supervisor-sprint-prompt.md](templates/supervisor-sprint-prompt.md): Sprint execution prompt template with placeholders for sprint plan, CLAUDE.md, llms.txt, baseline_status, enforcement (42 lines)
- [templates/replan-prompt.md](templates/replan-prompt.md): Replanner prompt template with placeholders for completed/remaining sprints (26 lines)
- [examples/sprint-queue-example.json](examples/sprint-queue-example.json): Queue file template for new users (42 lines)

## Tests

228 tests across 8 test files (~4780 lines total):
- [tests/test_supervisor.py](tests/test_supervisor.py): Core supervisor tests — sprint execution, worktree lifecycle, run loop (907 lines)
- [tests/test_integration.py](tests/test_integration.py): Integration tests — happy path, crash recovery, blocked sprints, retry scenarios (565 lines)
- [tests/test_queue.py](tests/test_queue.py): Queue tests — DAG resolution, atomic saves, status transitions (371 lines)
- [tests/test_notifications.py](tests/test_notifications.py): Notification tests — all 16 event types, Telegram API, stdout fallback (337 lines)
- [tests/test_replanner.py](tests/test_replanner.py): Replanner tests — JSON parsing, sprint modification, guard conditions (296 lines)
- [tests/test_parallel.py](tests/test_parallel.py): Parallel execution tests — concurrency, queue locking, dependency handling (219 lines)
- [tests/test_cli.py](tests/test_cli.py): CLI argument parsing and command dispatch tests (166 lines)
- [tests/test_checkpoint.py](tests/test_checkpoint.py): Checkpoint save/load and atomic write tests (87 lines)

## Key Concepts

- **2-agent specialized review:** Every PR is reviewed by 2 agents in parallel — Claude (Product lens: spec fit, user scenarios, data integrity) + Codex/secondary (Technical lens: correctness, security, architecture). Specialize, don't duplicate
- **Review gate:** Mandatory gate before every PR push — all reviewers must return ACCEPTED, evidence written to `.par-evidence.json`
- **Final Holistic Review:** After all sprints complete, two Opus reviewers (Technical + Product) review ALL code together to catch cross-module issues
- **Breakage Scenario:** Every review finding must include a concrete scenario where the issue causes a real problem — no scenario = not a finding
- **Compaction resilience:** Enforcement rules live in `~/.claude/rules/` (auto-loaded by Claude Code), phase docs are re-read from disk at boundaries
- **Markers:** `<!-- updated-by-superflow:YYYY-MM-DD -->` in generated files — used to detect whether Phase 0 has run
- **Sprint queue:** JSON file defining sprints with DAG dependencies, processed by the supervisor CLI
- **Checkpoints:** Per-sprint JSON files storing execution state, enabling crash recovery via `resume` command
- **Enforcement gates:** Validation gates (PAR evidence, sprint summary, verdict) that block progression until evidence is valid
- **Baseline test gate:** Heuristic test command detection per language — fails fast on broken baseline before implementation begins

<!-- updated-by-superflow:2026-03-24 -->
